features.evidence: 3 standards and finally this is a 14in laptop now the pavilion plus directly competes with the likes of the inspiron plus and the idea pad pro both of which we reviewed on this channel not too long ago so let s see if this laptop can hold its ground and what you can expect in terms of packaging the pavilion plus comes in a pretty standard looking cardboard box with a little bit of branding anyway open it up behind some protective packaging first and foremost you have the pavilion plus itself but we ll come back to that shortly now you also have the inclusion of a 100 w charging adapter which is quite interesting mind you also a fully braided type c cable then you have the wall out charging cable piece and then finally some basic documentation the pavilion plus is a solid looking laptop design wise it looks very similar to the base pavilion series it has those same rounded edges and the dimensions are generally the same it s symmetrical as well and look however it feels a lot closer to a higher end env series thanks to a nearly full metallic exterior which really gives it a nice premium feel now you also have h s iconic silver color that they use with a lot of their higher end laptops which is nice to see and then of course the laptop has a weight of just 3

8k resolution a variable refresh up to 120 hz a 16 x10 aspect ratio a peak wrens up to 400 ns for sdr content and up to 500 nits for hdr content which is plenty to overcome the glare from the glossy display we have here color rating is just as impressive with a 100 dci p3 color gamut coverage colors look sharp vivid and will be great for most general creative purposes today s video is in partnership with bangs the all in one infinti charging station is perfect if you want to be able to charge your smartphone smartwatch and headphones at the exact same time additionally thanks to a fully adjustable magnetic mount you can adjust the angle of your phone charger to get the optimal viewing angle you also have a nice convenient ld indicator letting when all your excesses are charging and the whole thing is powered by a single type c connection i also love the fact that it s got a clean minimalist design available in white or black which looks great across your bedside stand or on a professional work desk if you want to learn more about the infinity charger stand i will leave link in the video description below in terms of performance a quick recap of the specs we have on board so we ve got intel s core 5 ultra meteor lake chip we ve also got 16 gb of lpddr 5x memory and we also of course have intel s integrated graphics now you can upgrade to a more powerful core 7 ultra processor and 32 gb if your wallet has the capacity day to day activities surfing the web or crunching word documents or excel files is a breeze for this machine naturally more diand activities programming and code compilation is very much possible and this device is quite proficient thanks to the ample amount of processing power and system memory you can even get away with 3d level studio animations though with the lack of a discrete gpu i would not recommend it in a full time capacity more demanding activities multi layer 4k video editing while possible is not recommended you ll find if you re to editing more than two layers and eventually resolve you re going to start getting frame drops and a little bit of performance reduction i would upgrade to the higher end core 7 ultra model if that s something you want to do in a greater capacity now gaming performance on here is quite impressive compared to the fact that it s not a gaming laptop so games fortnite for example can run at a comfortable 60 frames per second on medium settings without a discrete gpu those numbers are quite impressive gone are the days when having an intel processor in your laptop meant setting it on fire cooler thermals have finally arrived so under unrealistic peak loads this laptop hit a maximum average surface temperature of 40 c which is about 4 cooler than the previous generation though more realistic sustain loads will only yield you around 37 c which is a very reasonable and cool number now as far as fan noise is concerned you can hit a maximum noise level of 55 db however that s only when you re running this laptop at peak capacity under more isle workloads the fan rarely goes on and generally speaking this is a pretty quiet machine self upgradability options are very limited so ram is entirely soldered on board meaning you re stuck with the configuration you get so make sure you get the right amount from the get go now you can upgrade the existing storage thanks to one available pcie gen 4 nvme slot though that s already occupied by m

3 standards and finally this is a 14in laptop now the pavilion plus directly competes with the likes of the inspiron plus and the idea pad pro both of which we reviewed on this channel not too long ago so let s see if this laptop can hold its ground and what you can expect in terms of packaging the pavilion plus comes in a pretty standard looking cardboard box with a little bit of branding anyway open it up behind some protective packaging first and foremost you have the pavilion plus itself but we ll come back to that shortly now you also have the inclusion of a 100 w charging adapter which is quite interesting mind you also a fully braided type c cable then you have the wall out charging cable piece and then finally some basic documentation the pavilion plus is a solid looking laptop design wise it looks very similar to the base pavilion series it has those same rounded edges and the dimensions are generally the same it s symmetrical as well and look however it feels a lot closer to a higher end env series thanks to a nearly full metallic exterior which really gives it a nice premium feel now you also have h s iconic silver color that they use with a lot of their higher end laptops which is nice to see and then of course the laptop has a weight of just 3
1 port we have two type c ports one of which supports thunderbolt 4 and the second one has power delivery as well as display port 2

this right here is hp s latest pavilion plus4 laptop and it s classified as a upper mid range laptop to me it s wild that in 2024 we have an entire society and hierarchy system of laptops there s budget mid range upper mid range premium upper premium flag it s just wild anyway the latest iteration of the pavilon plus 14 is rocking intel s core 5 ultra processor 16 gb of lpddr5 5x memory we also have a nice large 1 tbte ssd additionally we do have intel s latest generation of integrated arc graphics combined with wi fi 6c and bluetooth 5

2 drive so you ll have to replace the existing one you ve got a modest 68w hour battery here which will get you a comfortable 9 hours of real world usage time with generally light load activities though of course that will vary depending on what you re doing now the speaker quality here is some of the worst i ve heard on pretty much any laptop i ve reviewed this year this thing is worse than a lot of budget laptops out there there s no depth there s a lot of distortion even at lower volume levels and they don t get very loud either here s a quick sound test for reference yo stand up we going to make some moves tonight yeah stand up got something to prove tonight so stand up we ain t going to plan tonight we going to take action on our dreams and fight all right we ain t going to st never stop we going to make it all to the to with a price tech just shy of 1,000 for the configuration we have here it s no secret that this laptop is treading very close to premium territory however hp brings a lot to the table here you ve got a sturdy build over here with a lot of premium materials to give you a nice feel for the machine additionally thanks to a fantastic display everything looks flush and vivid you ve also got great input devices thanks to a great keyboard and decent trackpad iort standards are some of the best you ll find in class and i absolutely love the fact that performance and thermals are generally speaking getting better year over year now the laptop isn t perfect battery life and speakers could be a little bit better in fact the speakers could definitely be better but despite these compromises i think the pavilion plus is probably one of the best laptops you can get right now it brings a lot of value and almost makes you slightly more expensive envy entirely useless because you re getting similar configurations for a ultimately lower price let me know what you think of the new pavilion plus 14 if you are going to be getting this laptop i d love to hear your thoughts on it i will leave links to the product in the video description below and as always if you enjoyed the content make sure you hit that button and consider subscribing to our channel it genuinely helps us grow catch you in the next one

3 standards and finally this is a 14in laptop now the pavilion plus directly competes with the likes of the inspiron plus and the idea pad pro both of which we reviewed on this channel not too long ago so let s see if this laptop can hold its ground and what you can expect in terms of packaging the pavilion plus comes in a pretty standard looking cardboard box with a little bit of branding anyway open it up behind some protective packaging first and foremost you have the pavilion plus itself but we ll come back to that shortly now you also have the inclusion of a 100 w charging adapter which is quite interesting mind you also a fully braided type c cable then you have the wall out charging cable piece and then finally some basic documentation the pavilion plus is a solid looking laptop design wise it looks very similar to the base pavilion series it has those same rounded edges and the dimensions are generally the same it s symmetrical as well and look however it feels a lot closer to a higher end env series thanks to a nearly full metallic exterior which really gives it a nice premium feel now you also have h s iconic silver color that they use with a lot of their higher end laptops which is nice to see and then of course the laptop has a weight of just 3

1 functionality which is amazing on the other side one more usba a super speed port and of course the good old headphone jack the only thing really missing here unfortunately is the inclusion of an sd card reader slot which would have made the whole package perfect but nonetheless standards are pretty good as they are the bottom of the laptop is removable you ll notice you have a nice large air intake fent over here which is nice to see and then additionally you have a couple rubber grips to keep the laptop in place and finally if you look at the bottom side you ll notice on each side you have a speaker grill indicating this is a bottom firing setup unfolding this laptop presents you with a very elegant looking inner chassis it s all metallic in terms of the build quality here again and then additionally i appreciate the fact that that silver color really compliments the general look of this machine now at the center you have a nice large trackpad look at all that serve real estate space the only real downside here is that it is ultimately still a plastic surface finish which means inevitably you are going to have a little bit of flex but it seems to be mostly under control generally speaking it s a tactile trackpad and not bad to use hp has been steadily increasing their keyboard quality with a lot of their laptops over the years but this right here is the perfect iteration you ve got nice large key caps i love the gry tone look over here the font is large and easy to read additionally the key caps are very sturdy in their general quality i also appreciate the fact the keyboard is fully backed with multiple brightness settings since this is a 14 in keyboard you re not going to get a 10 keypad over here however you ll be happy to know the actual quality of the typing experience is fantastic you have a ton of key travel per key cap every keystroke feels tactile and the result is a very high quality and ultimately premium feeling typing experience unfortunately the same cannot be said about the hinge quality so hp has opted use a single tear hinge mechanism which exerts a ton of force on the body my concern here is that if you re not gentle with the hinge over the years you may be prone to damage from wear and tear display fitting does have a plastic enc casing all around however what s again not so nice here is that you do have a pretty noticeable chin at the bottom and for no good reason they could have just trimmed out the hp branding and made it a little bit slimmer the bezels on the other hand are nice narrow within modern day standards and then at the top again you do have a larger than average forehead over here which does take away from the immersion the good news is that at the center you have a highquality 5 megapixel webcam with a built in ir scanner and dual array microphone system depending on where you are in the world you have the option to choose between two distinct display types one being the standard qhd plus ips panel the other one being the higher end display which we have in this configuration so it s a 14 in oled panel with a 2
